query: Impact on wildlife and ecosystem
reference_summary: Explosion and fire on the BP-licensed Transocean drilling rig Deepwater Horizon in the Gulf of Mexico .The Deepwater Horizon oil spill severely disrupted fisheries and fishing communities in the Gulf of Mexico. On May 2, 2010, the federal government closed 3% of federal Gulf waters to fishing, a restriction that expanded significantly over the following weeks. By June 2, 37% of federal Gulf waters were closed, marking the peak of fishing closures due to oil contamination. The closures were prompted by health and environmental concerns as oil continued to gush from the well at rates later estimated to reach up to 70,000 barrels per day, with 4.9 million barrels ultimately spilled by the time the well was sealed in July 2010.The oil reached coastal wetlands and shorelines across all five Gulf Coast states, further endangering marine life and disrupting local fisheries. Notably, toxic oil and dispersants affected sensitive ecosystems such as the Chandeleur Islands, critical nesting sites for birds and nursery areas for fish. On August 3, the U.S. government officially declared it the largest offshore oil spill in history, and scientists later estimated that only about 20% of the oil was recovered.Economic consequences were profound. BP began paying compensation claims, including $147 million by July 5 and $319 million by August 9, to those affected, including fishers. BP ultimately agreed to establish a $20 billion compensation fund on June 17. Despite cleanup progress, concerns about lingering contamination remained: tar balls continued to appear in fishing areas even seven months later, and the dwarf seahorse, a species reliant on Gulf seagrasses, was pushed toward possible extinction.The disruption not only impacted fishing income but also led to long-term distrust in offshore drilling regulations and responses, as highlighted in congressional investigations and government reports criticizing both BP and federal oversight. The spill also triggered a ban on new drilling, later lifted with stricter oversight, but fishing communities remained wary of the lasting environmental and economic toll.

query: Financial Impacts on BP

query: BP’s Compensation, cost and fine
reference_summary: Explosion and fire on the BP-licensed Transocean drilling rig Deepwater Horizon in the Gulf of Mexico .BP executives face Congress in a closed session , as the White House backs a Senate proposal to increase the limit on liability payouts from $ 75m to $ 10bn -LRB- ¬ # 6.5 bn -RRB- for the cost of a spill .BP agrees to a $ 20bn -LRB- ¬ # 13.5 bn -RRB- downpayment towards compensation for victims of the oil spill .Legislation introduced into the Senate by Democrats to cap oil spill compensation claims at $ 75m has been stopped because there was n't enough support from within the party .The US campaign group Public Citizen also calls off its boycott of BP , and some analysts predict that the company 's legal costs could be well below the ¬ # 13bn it has set aside .BP increases the estimated total cost of the oil spill to $ 40bn , $ 7.7 bn more than previously expected .BP oil spill costs to hit $ 40bn.Ken Feinberg expects to pay out only $ 2.3 bn in emergency claims over Gulf of Mexico oil spill from $ 20bn fund Half of BP oill spill damages claims  inadequate ' , says payout chief.A few days before the trial was to begin , BP and lawyers for the plaintiffs in a trial over the 2010 oil spill reach a settlement .Tens of thousands of people waiting for money from a fund being managed by Ken Feinberg , the Gulf Coast Claims Facility , can take what the settlement offers them or opt out and make a claim directly to a BP-run entity .They can sue if they do n't like what they get from that fund .BP agrees to pay $ 4.5 billion in a settlement with the U.S. government over the massive 2010 oil spill and to plead guilty to felony counts related to the deaths of 11 workers and lying to Congress .The figure includes nearly $ 1.3 billion in criminal fines  the largest such penalty ever along with payments to several government entities .

query: The drilling licensing issue
reference_summary: An explosion aboard the Deepwater Horizon drilling rig in the Gulf of Mexico , 52 miles -LRB- 84km -RRB- south-east of Venice , Louisiana , kills 11 workers. The rig was drilling in about 5,000 ft -LRB- 1,525 m -RRB- of water , pushing the boundaries of deepwater drilling technology .President Barack Obama 's administration bans oil drilling in new areas off the US coast pending investigations into the cause of the BP spill .President Obama announces a six-month moratorium on new deepwater drilling in the gulf .Before the spill , Mr Obama had said he would allow new offshore drilling .Obama orders Salazar to complete report on safety measures for offshore drilling within 30 days , says no new leases will go forward without needed safeguards .MMS approves plan for two relief wells .US bans new drilling in Gulf of Mexico The US justice department announces that a team of lawyers is monitoring the spill . Safety inspections of all 30 deepwater drilling rigs and 47 deepwater production platforms are ordered .A US appeals court rejects the federal government 's effort to restore an offshore deepwater drilling moratorium , opening the door to resumed drilling in the Gulf of Mexico while the legal fight continues .The same appeals court is expected to hear arguments on the merits of the moratorium case in late August or early September .US appeals court opens door to new drilling in Gulf of Mexico. BP suggests it may return for oil to the , recently leaking , Macondo well .Obama 's administration responds by saying such a decision would have to be made by well licensing authorities , rather than BP . The exchange is the latest in an ongoing struggle between BP and the US government .BP risks Obama row by hinting it may return to stricken oil well.BP 's poor reputation , following the Gulf of Mexico oil spill , forces it to pull out of a bid to drill in the Arctic .BP frozen out of Arctic oil drilling race.The Obama administration 's six-month moratorium on new deepwater drilling operations ends .Environmental groups express concern over the decision , while supporters of offshore drilling dismiss the announcement , arguing that the moratorium is effectively still in place due to continuing uncertainty over new drilling regulations .US drops oil drilling moratorium.

query: Storms and Hurricanes inference
reference_summary: An explosion aboard the Deepwater Horizon drilling rig in the Gulf of Mexico , 52 miles -LRB- 84km -RRB- south-east of Venice , Louisiana , kills 11 workers .Dozens of vessels , including the rig drilling a relief well to permanently block the damaged well , are ordered to leave the site as Tropical Storm Bonnie approaches .', 'BP warns that the final operation to plug the well could be delayed by up to two weeks by the storm .', 'The capped well is to remain unmonitored for several days .Ships involved in BP 's effort to secure the blown-out oil well prepare to resume work after a tropical storm in the Gulf of Mexico weakened .Coast Guard chief Adm Thad Allen says the storm put back efforts to drill a relief well by seven to 10 days .Hurricane Alex causes heavy seas , disrupting BP 's clean-up efforts .First hurricane of season hits BP oil spill clean-up.As the storm passes , crews return to the site in order to finish work on relief wells before the hurricane season starts .Efforts to plug BP oil leak resume after storm passes. BP suspends drilling of relief well to allow a tropical storm , believed to be heading for the Gulf of Mexico , to pass .Tropical storm forces BP to halt relief well drilling.

query: Tony Hayward and BP’s Congressional Testimony
reference_summary: An explosion aboard the Deepwater Horizon drilling rig in the Gulf of Mexico , 52 miles -LRB- 84km -RRB- south-east of Venice , Louisiana , kills 11 workers .At a series of congressional hearings , BP , Transocean and Halliburton , the three companies involved in the Deepwater Horizon drilling operations , all blame each other for the disaster .Hayward is accused by members of the US Congress of ` stonewalling '' after failing to answer a series of questions about the spill 's causes .Tony Hayward stonewalls Congress. BP chief executive Tony Hayward receives a tongue-lashing at a hearing in the US Congress . Bill Lehr , a senior scientist at the NOAA , disputes White House claims that the worst of the oil spill is over , claiming that three-quarters of the oil remains in the Gulf environment .Under questioning at a Congress committee hearing , Lehr contradicted an earlier report in which he had stated that most of the oil had been cleaned up .

query: BP‘s Efforts to Kill the spill
reference_summary: An explosion aboard the Deepwater Horizon drilling rig in the Gulf of Mexico , 52 miles -LRB- 84km -RRB- south-east of Venice , Louisiana , kills 11 workers .Five missions disperse about 13,000 gallons of chemical dispersants .10 missions disperse roughly 15,000 additional gallons of dispersants , and 21,000 feet of containment boom are placed at the spill site .BP 's attempts to repair hydraulic leak on the blowout preventer valve unsuccessful Homeland Security report projects that based on drift calculations.BP 's efforts to contain the leak.BP engineers use undersea robots to move the containment chamber over the larger of the two remaining leaks on the seabed .This containment method has never before been attempted at such a depth . Efforts to close valves on a failed blowout preventer with underwater robots are abandoned .Deepwater team attempts to put 100-tonne box over blown-out oil well Video recreation of cofferdam method.BP 's effort to place a giant metal box atop the leaking well to contain the spill fails when ice crystals accumulate inside the box and engineers are forced to remove it .BP 's containment dome hits a snag when a buildup of crystallised gas forces engineers to postpone efforts to place the chamber over the oil leak and draw the oil to the surface .Quick fix dashed as BP tower fails to contain oil.BP officials weigh shoving debris , including golf balls and rubber tyres , into the leaking wellhead , a maneuver known as the  junk shot  .They also ready a  top hat '' - a metal dome - to be placed over the leak .After the failure of the four-storey-high dome to draw oil from the major leak , BP lowers a five-foot-high dome to attempt to cover the smaller leak .BP plans to insert a 4in-tube into the ruptured 21in riser pipe that would take the oil to the surface .If that fails , they will use the small containment dome that has already been lowered .Both methods are intended to reduce , not to stop , the leak .The tube begins to draw oil to the surface .The volume of oil traveling through the tube is gradually increased to avoid the hydrate formation that doomed the large containment dome .The tube inserted into the leaking pipe captures much less oil than expected .BP admits Deepwater rescue is capturing less oil.BP prepares to plug the leaking well with heavy drilling mud , a procedure called a  top kill .The attempt is declared a failure three days later .BP places a cap , called the  lower marine riser package , atop the leaking wellhead .The cap allows the company to pipe much of the oil and gas leaking from the well to ships on the surface .Attempts to place a cap over the valves that were meant to prevent the rig from leaking show signs of success .BP begins a bid to place a tighter-fitting cap atop the leaking wellhead .The company warns that oil will flow freely while the caps are being exchanged , but says it has brought in 400 oil-skimming ships to deal with the increased flow .BP begin their latest attempt to seal the leak .Robots remove a leaking cap from the well , to allow a replacement containment system to be installed .BP begins new operation to seal off leaking Deepwater Horizon well.the latest attempt to stem the leak reportedly going well.BP successfully installs a new , more tightly fitting containment cap on the ruptured wellhead .The next step is to test the internal pressure in the well to establish whether the flow has been stopped .New cap  successfully installed ' on leaking well.BP stops the flow of oil for the first time in 87 days , raising hopes that it could be sealed off for good. 'BP stops oil leak in Gulf of Mexico for first time since April.Meanwhile , BP says it has been given permission to prepare for a  static kill '' - pumping mud into the top of the well through the new cap - a step viewed as an intermediate measure .BP will attempt to stem the flow of oil with a  static kill ' in the next 24 hours .The procedure involves pumping heavy drilling mud and cement into the well .BP  static kill ' due in next 24 hours.BP sprayed almost 2m gallons of Corexit on the slick and at the leak site on the seabed .Meanwhile , BP reports  encouraging '' progress with the  static kill '' operation to plug the well with mud and seal it with cement .BP pumps cement into blown-out oil well to seal it for good.BP pumps cement to seal the damaged well after it was intercepted by a relief well .BP finally seals off the ruptured well .

query: BP’s Attempts Using static kill and top kill
reference_summary: An explosion aboard the Deepwater Horizon drilling rig in the Gulf of Mexico , 52 miles -LRB- 84km -RRB- south-east of Venice , Louisiana , kills 11 workers . Meanwhile , BP sends undersea robots to the wellhead in an unsuccessful effort to activate the blowout preventer , a piece of heavy kit mounted on top of the well to stem the flow of oil.BP tries to stop the spill by lowering a 98-ton ` containment dome '' over the leak .The effort eventually fails , as crystallized gases cause the containment dome to become unexpectedly buoyant .BP 's effort to place a giant metal box atop the leaking well to contain the spill fails when ice crystals accumulate inside the box and engineers are forced to remove it .BP announces plans to place a small containment dome , known as a  top hat  , over the blown-out well to funnel oil to the surface. BP begins  top kill '' attempt , shooting mud down the drillpipe in an attempt to clog the leaking well .After several days , the effort is abandoned .Hayward says the  top kill '' effort to plug the well is progressing as planned and had a 60 to 70 percent chance of success , the same odds he gave before the maneuver .Meanwhile , BP says it has been given permission to prepare for a  static kill  - pumping mud into the top of the well through the new cap - a step viewed as an intermediate measure .BP prepares to plug the leaking well with heavy drilling mud , a procedure called a  top kill .The attempt is declared a failure three days later . Meanwhile , BP says it has been given permission to prepare for a  static kill '' - pumping mud into the top of the well through the new cap - a step viewed as an intermediate measure .BP will attempt to stem the flow of oil with a  static kill ' in the next 24 hours .The procedure involves pumping heavy drilling mud and cement into the well .BP  static kill ' due in next 24 hours. Meanwhile , BP reports  encouraging '' progress with the  static kill '' operation to plug the well with mud and seal it with cement .

query: Oil Flow Estimates and Leak Rate Revisions
reference_summary: An explosion aboard the Deepwater Horizon drilling rig in the Gulf of Mexico , 52 miles -LRB- 84km -RRB- south-east of Venice , Louisiana , kills 11 workers .A Coast Guard official says the Macondo well , which the rig had been drilling , could be releasing up to 8,000 barrels of oil per day .Oil leak is estimated at 1,000 barrels a day -LRB- 42,000 gallons -RRB- .''Homeland Security report says estimated discharge rate could reach 64,000 to 110,000 barrels of oil per day if were to completely blow out .In a reverse , officials reveal the well is leaking an estimated 1,000 barrels of oil per day and warn of environmental disaster .The US Coast Guard warns the oil leak could become the worst oil spill in US history .A third oil leak is discovered , and the Coast Guard revises estimates of the leak from 1,000 to 5,000 barrels of oil a day .Meanwhile , officials revise the estimate of the leak 's rate upward to 5,000 barrels per day . Steve Wereley , a researcher at Purdue University , tells the press he believes the well is leaking 70,000 bpd .Researchers who have analyzed underwater video from the leak site estimate as many as 70,000 barrels of oil are leaking into the Gulf per day , with a margin of error of plus or minus 20 % , significantly higher than earlier estimates .BP reports that the amount of oil drawn off by the tube has increased to 5,000 bpd .Given that BP had previously estimated the entire leak at 5,000 bpd and the tube only draws off a fraction of the spill , the leak must be much larger than previously estimated .Experts testifying at the congressional hearing put the figure at 20,000-100 ,000 barrels per day .Carol Browner , President Barack Obama 's adviser on energy policy , says the spill is the worst environmental disaster in US history , worse even than the 1989 Exxon Valdez spill in Alaska .The US Geological Survey estimates the oil flow at as many as 40,000 barrels per day before a cap was put on the well on 3 June .Scientists double their estimate of the scale of the spill to 40,000 barrels a day .Scale of BP oil leak revised up to 40,000 barrels a day.Based on the high end of government estimates , the spill surpasses the 140 million gallon mark .The government estimates that 4.9 million barrels -LRB- 205.8 million gallons -RRB- leaked , 19 times the size of the Exxon Valdez spill .US scientists say the oil from the well has cleared from the sea surface faster than expected , 100 days after the disaster began .The US government says the oil spill is officially the biggest leak ever , with 4.9 million barrels of oil leaked before the well was capped last month .Scientists estimate that a total of 4.4 m barrels of oil were released into the Gulf of Mexico during the nearly three months the well leaked .BP spill released 4.4 m barrels of oil into the ocean , study finds.
